rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the component type scope when contracts relate to component ports
Use implementation scopes for annex blocks that reference subcomponent ports or connections
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only adding annex blocks, not modifying existing elements
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annex blocks
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by only inserting annex blocks, not modifying existing AADL elements
Preserve existing component types and implementations by only adding annex blocks
Use visible identifiers when available to reference ports and other features in annex blocks
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use `assume` and `guarantee` for contracts, `eq` for equations, and `assign` for bindings
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that reference subcomponent ports in component type annex blocks
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as ports or constants
Do not modify existing AADL elements when inserting annex blocks
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annex blocks
